title: fix(docs): Fix glossary FIXME tags
body:
<!--
Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #31628 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.


#### Any other comments?
While working on [#31628](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/31628), I encountered a `ModuleNotFoundError` due to a misnamed Sphinx extension (`sphinx-prompt` instead of `sphinx_prompt`). This PR also fixes that to ensure local doc builds (`make`) work correctly.

#### Setup
```
Python 3.13.3

   machine: macOS-15.5-arm64-arm-64bit-Mach-O

Python dependencies:
      sklearn: 1.8.dev0
          pip: 25.0.1
   setuptools: None
        numpy: 2.3.1
        scipy: 1.16.0
       Cython: 3.1.2
       pandas: 2.3.0
   matplotlib: 3.10.3
       joblib: 1.5.1
threadpoolctl: 3.6.0

Built with OpenMP: False
```

step_plan.summary: Fix glossary FIXME tags by rewording offending lines in doc/glossary.rst and correct a misnamed Sphinx extension in doc/conf.py (change "sphinx-prompt" to "sphinx_prompt") so local doc builds do not raise ModuleNotFoundError.

steps.operation: Fix Sphinx extension name in configuration
steps.reason: The extensions list currently references the wrong package name "sphinx-prompt", which causes a ModuleNotFoundError when building the docs locally. Replace it with the correct import name "sphinx_prompt" to match the actual Python package/module name.
steps.side_effects: Removes the ModuleNotFoundError during local doc builds; requires that the environment has the sphinx_prompt package installed under that name. No API or runtime code changes. Doc build/test jobs should be re-run; CI that builds the docs will be affected (should pass). If any custom tooling referenced the hyphenated name, update it accordingly.

steps.operation: Replace FIXME tags in glossary wording
steps.reason: Remove 'FIXME' placeholders and replace them with final, neutral phrasing so the documentation contains no FIXME warnings and reads clearly. Example: change the parenthetical note about cloning in the 'meta-estimator' entry from '(although FIXME: Pipeline and FeatureUnion do not do this currently)' to a neutral note such as '(Note: Pipeline and FeatureUnion currently do not perform this cloning).'.
steps.side_effects: Changes user-visible documentation text; verify factual accuracy (ensure Pipeline and FeatureUnion behavior is still as stated). Documentation tests and grep-for-FIXME checks should pass. Rendering of the glossary page will change slightly — reviewers should verify grammar and meaning. No effect on runtime code or public APIs.
